reason: Sample shows ~36/150 mismatches (exact_match ~0.76), about 3 SE below the claimed 0.8567.
12 false negatives against ~55 true positives in the sample gives recall ~0.82, far below the claimed 0.92 which would predict only ~5-6 FNs.
36 errors in 150 sampled rows gives accuracy ~0.760, fully consistent with the claimed 0.7697 over 178 rows.
Sample returns sum to ~+0.078 (mean ~0.0005/day), extrapolating to ~25-30% total return over 500 days, well short of the claimed 82.9% which needs ~0.0012/day.
All 42 rows shown: 11x5000 minus 9x3000 plus the 22 remaining trades nets exactly 19024.77, matching the claim to the cent.
~34 errors in the 150-row sample gives accuracy ~0.77, about 2.5-3 SE below the claimed 0.8558.
Daily std is ~0.007 (annualized ~0.11); the claimed 0.0607 matches neither daily nor annualized convention and looks under-reported by roughly half.
7 errors in 150 sampled rows (4.7%) vs claimed 2.89% (expected ~4.3 errors); suggests under-reported error rate, though small counts make this uncertain.
~36/150 errors (accuracy ~0.76) with concentrated 3->8 and 8->9 confusions implies macro-F1 ~0.75-0.78, well below the claimed 0.8552.
Mean squared error across all 150 sampled rows is consistently ~0.146 (three 50-row blocks: 0.147/0.147/0.146), ~6% above the claimed 0.1382 - a subtle under-report, though near sampling noise.
